title: ulex europaeus var. remote-bracteatus
intro: Ulex europaeus var. remote-bracteatus, also known as the gorse, is a variant of the European gorse plant, characterized by its compact growth habit and remote bracts. The medicinal properties of Ulex europaeus var. remote-bracteatus are primarily attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which offer numerous health benefits such as reduced inflammation and improved immune function. Herbal preparations of Ulex europaeus var. remote-bracteatus are often made into teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can be used both internally and externally to support overall well-being.
